LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JOHN ERWIN, BAY COUNTIES ELECTRIC INC, AND, SURETY COMPANY OF THE PACIFIC DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED IN PART, AND OVERRULED IN PART. DEMURRER IS OVERRULED AS TO 1ST, 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION. THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 2ND, 5TH, 6TH, 8TH, 9TH, AND 10TH CAUSES OF ACTION. A BREACH OF CONTRACT SHOULD BE ADDRESSED THROUGH A CONTRACT REMEDY RATHER THAN A TORT REMEDY. EQUITABLE INDEMNITY IS ONLY AVAILABLE AMONG TORT FEASORS WHO ARE JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY LIABLE FOR A PLAINTIFF'S INJURY. THE RIGHT TO IMPLIED CONTRACTUAL INDEMNITY IS PREDICATED ON INDEMNITOR'S BREACH OF CONTRACT. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE HOW ERWIN'S ASSIGNMENT OF RIGHTS COULD CONSTITUTE A BREACH OF THE UNDERLYING CONTRACT WITH PLAINTIFF. 8TH, 9TH, AND 10TH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUBJECT TO THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS REGARDING INTERFERENCE UNRELATED TO LIEN AND OTHER LITIGATION ACTIVITIES. ALSO, THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE COMPLAINT NO.485532. THE ORDER SHOULD INCLUDE THE COURT'S COMMENTS AT THE BEGINNING OF THE HEARING, AS WELL. (DEFENSE COUNSEL WILL PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-29-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JOHN ERWIN, BAY COUNTIES ELECTRIC INC, and, SURETY COMPANY OF THE PACIFIC DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Rick W. Grady, of Allen Matkins, (415) 273-8402, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; James. R. Busselle, Esq. of Leonidou & Rosin (650) 691-2888, appearing for defendants and moving party; Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained in part, and overruled in part. Demurrer is overruled as to 1st, 3rd causes of action. The 4th cause of action is sustained with ten days leave to amend. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the 2nd, 5th, 6th, 8th, 9th, and 10th causes of action. A breach of contract should be addressed through a contract remedy rather than a tort remedy. Equitable indemnity is only available among tort feasors who are jointly and severally liable for a plaintiff's injury. The right to implied contractual indemnity is predicated on indemnitor's breach of contract. Plaintiff fails to allege how Erwin's assignment of rights could constitute a breach of the underlying contract with plaintiff. 8th, 9th, and 10th causes of action are subject to the litigation privilege. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the 7th cause of action. Plaintiff to allege facts regarding interference unrelated to lien and other litigation activities. Also, the Court takes Judicial Notice of the Complaint No.485532. The order should include the Court's comments at the beginning of the hearing, as well. (Defense counsel will prepare Order.) (302/CWW/PB)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
